# Are Colors Defineable?

**Source:** [Color Nerd](https://www.youtube.com/@ColorNerd1) (YouTube Shorts)
**Date:** 2026-03-20
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/shorts/e33WEqkaPPQ
**Guest:** Kory Stamper (lexicographer, ex–Merriam-Webster)

## Description

What does it mean for a color to be "bluer than fiesta"? While working as a lexicographer at Merriam-Webster, Kory Stamper noticed something strange: the color definitions in Webster's Third New International Dictionary were whimsical and evocative. This led her down a rabbit hole into the world of mid-twentieth-century color science.

Kory's research led to a book: _True Color: The Strange and Spectacular Quest to Define Color — from Azure to Zinc Pink_ (out March 31, 2026 from Knopf).

## Transcript

How do you even define color terms? This is one of the two color plates in the second edition of Merriam-Webster. You get this really interesting and funny, like jazzy little thing going on.

> > Yep.

> > Like, what is happening? Right.

> > These color plates were one of the reasons why there was a big push into systematic color defining. My name is Kory Stamper. I am a lexicographer and an author. When I worked at Merriam-Webster, I ran across a definition for the color term "begonia." It was so bananas. So, I'll read it: "A deep pink that is bluer, lighter, and stronger than average coral. See coral 3B. Bluer than fiesta. And bluer and stronger than sweet William. Called also —" everything after "a deep pink" I was like —

> > What? What color is fiesta?

> > And I was like, who wrote these? This is a hue page. This is I.H. Godlove's handwriting. So, here's sweet William. Here's begonia.

> > Deep pink 3 is this guy right here.

> > There we go. The pace at which he was defining was insane. It was like in 7 months 3,000 definitions were written.

> > Margaret, his wife, was the one who was actually doing all the hands-on defining.

True Color: The Strange and Spectacular Quest to Define Color from Azure to Zinc Pink is out March 31st, 2026. It is for word nerds, color nerds, and just people who like weird stories.

## Key People & References

- **I.H. Godlove** — wrote ~3,000 color definitions for Webster's in 7 months; his wife Margaret did much of the hands-on defining work
- **Kory Stamper** — lexicographer, author of _True Color_ (Knopf, March 2026)
- **Inter-Society Color Council (ISCC)** — 1930s–1950s effort to systematize color naming
- **ISCC-NBS method** — standardized color designation system, divided color solid into 319 named blocks
